query: SpaceX Blue Origin commercial landers Artemis III 2027 low Earth orbit demonstration mission specifics

{"query":"SpaceX Blue Origin commercial landers Artemis III 2027 low Earth orbit demonstration mission specifics","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1511,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://satnews.com/2026/07/15/nasa-details-docking-and-software-protocols-for-revised-artemis-iii-leo-demonstration/","title":"NASA Details Docking and Software Protocols for Revised Artemis III LEO Demonstration – SatNews","content":"On Wednesday, July 15, 2026, NASA published new operational parameters for its upcoming Artemis III demonstration mission scheduled for 2027.
SpaceX Docking Mechanics: Orion will perform a nose-to-nose docking procedure with the Starlink-linked Starship Version 3 HLS test article."},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/artemis-campaign-development-division/human-landing-system-program/how-nasas-artemis-iii-lander-test-will-pave-way-for-moon-landings/","title":"How NASA’s Artemis III Lander Test Will Pave Way for Moon Landings - NASA","content":"Before Artemis astronauts land on the Moon’s surface in 2028, NASA will conduct the Artemis III demonstration mission in 2027, allowing teams on Earth and in orbit to practice rendezvous and docking operations between commercial human landing systems and the Orion spacecraft.
“Each human landing system provider has taken a different approach to the Artemis III mission,” said Steve Creech, program manager, Human Landing System Program, NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama.
Blue Origin’s lander test article is planned to launch first and will be able to loiter in space for up to 30 days, allowing for checkouts in orbit prior to the launch of SLS and Orion from Launch Complex 39B at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida."},{"url":"https://www.space.com/space-exploration/artemis/full-steam-ahead-nasa-ramps-up-prep-for-artemis-iii-astronaut-launch-in-2027","title":"'Full steam ahead': NASA ramps up prep for Artemis III astronaut launch in 2027 | Space","content":"NASA is targeting mid-to-late 2027 for Artemis III, which will launch four astronauts into low Earth orbit (LEO) aboard the Orion spacecraft.
Several components for the mission's SLS rocket have arrived in the Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) at KSC.
Josh has covered the evolution of NASA's commercial spaceflight partnerships and crewed missions from the Space Coast, NASA science missions and more."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artemis_III","title":"Artemis III - Wikipedia","content":"Planned for 2027, the mission is designed as a demonstration flight in low Earth orbit rather than a lunar landing.
Prior to February 2026, Artemis III had the role of Artemis IV.
After separation, Orion's European Service Module would place the spacecraft into a circular orbit approximately 230 nautical miles (430 km; 260 mi) above Earth.
The four prime crew members were assigned as Randy Bresnik, commander; Luca Parmitano, pilot; Frank Rubio, mission specialist; and Andre Douglas, mission specialist.
There are no women on the crew, a decision that NASA administrator Jared Isaacman acknowledged had drawn "reactions ranging from disappointment to outrage", but stated that the selected crew members were, "the best astronauts to undertake and complete the mission's objectives".
The European Service Module (ESM) for Artemis III, ESM-3, was delivered to NASA from the Airbus facility in Bremen, Germany in September 2024.
The spacecraft's pressure vessel was built by Lockheed Martin at the Michoud Assembly Facility and shipped to the Kennedy Space Center in August 2021 for final assembly."},{"url":"https://payloadspace.com/artemis-iii-astronauts-will-meet-spacex-blue-origin-landers-in-earth-orbit/","title":"Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit","content":"SpaceX and Blue Origin plan to each provide a lander for the 2027 mission to Earth orbit, which will serve as a test run for Moon landings starting as soon as the following year, NASA confirmed at a Tuesday event.
ESA Pilot Luca Parmitano, a two-time ISS astronaut and past ISS commander who once survived a water incursion during a spacewalk.
NASA Mission Specialist Frank Rubio, a one-time ISS astronaut who saw his mission unexpectedly double to a US-record-breaking 371 days due to spacecraft problems."}]}

Four astronauts will launch aboard","score":0.7923522,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://payloadspace.com/artemis-iii-astronauts-will-meet-spacex-blue-origin-landers-in-earth-orbit","title":"Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin ...","content":"# Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit. SpaceX and Blue Origin plan to each provide a lander for the 2027 mission to Earth orbit, which will serve as a test run for Moon landings starting as soon as the following year, NASA confirmed at a Tuesday event. **Best of both worlds:** During the two-week Artemis III mission, if all goes to plan, astronauts will meet up first with Blue Origin’s Mark 1 and then, SpaceX’s Starship V3. The Orion spacecraft will dock with each lander in turn, and on Mark 1, crew will open the hatch in a test run for future lunar ops. The space agency tapped SpaceX to provide Starship for the historic touchdown, although NASA has said it may pivot to Blue Origin to speed things up. * SpaceX VP Jessica Jensen said Starship would fly the crucial trans-lunar injection on the Artemis IV landing mission, instead of using Orion like Artemis II’s crew.","score":0.77994305,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/ScientificAmerican/posts/in-2027s-artemis-iii-mission-the-space-agency-aims-to-test-two-challengers-space/1344854927512776","title":"In 2027's Artemis III mission, the space agency aims to test two challengers ...","content":"It is now planned as a low-Earth orbit test mission around mid-2027 to demonstrate rendezvous, docking with one or both commercial landers","score":0.767429,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.artemisfiles.com","title":"Artemis 3","content":"Artemis III will now demonstrate critical rendezvous and docking capabilities in low Earth orbit.
